Viscoelasticity of biofilms and their recalcitrance to mechanical and chemical challenges

evidence_entities.text: In the medical arena, it is currently estimated that over 60% of all human infections treated by physicians are due to biofilms
This matrix not only yields bacterial phenotypes that can be different from their planktonic counterparts, but also offers physical protection against mechanical and chemical challenges
Penetration of chlorhexidine into oral biofilms increased with increasing relative importance of the slow and decreasing importance of the fast relaxation element
Gentamicin has been shown to have enhanced killing in P. aeruginosa and Escherichia coli biofilms in vitro and in vivo in rabbits after ultrasound treatment
Pseudomonas aeruginosa biofilms on stainless steel were affected by N-acetyl-L-cysteine, as both the fast and intermediate Maxwell elements were reduced by more than 50%
